name: Kasseri Tomato & Basil Cresents (Kasseropites
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, blend together the warm water, olive oil, red wine vinegar, egg, and salt until well combined.
2. Gradually stir in enough flour so that the dough pulls away from the sides of the bowl.
3. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.
4. Shape the dough into a round ball. Grease a bowl with oil.
5. Place the dough into the greased bowl and turn to coat the surface. Cover with a damp towel and let rise in a warm, draft-free area for 30 minutes.
6. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
7. In a bowl, combine the Kasseri cheese, chopped tomatoes, fresh basil, and paprika.
8. Divide the dough into 8 pieces. Shape each piece into a smooth round ball.
9. Roll each ball of dough on a lightly floured surface until it is about 1/4 to 1/8 inch thick.
10. Spoon some of the cheese mixture down the center of each round.
11. Fold one side over the filling and press the edges together to seal.
12. Arrange the cresents on prepared baking sheets.
13. Sprinkle with paprika.
14. Bake until the cheese has melted and the pastry is golden brown, about 40 minutes. Serve hot.
